# terraform-module-mssql-management [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mssql-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mssql-management/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mssql-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mssql-management/commits)


Cloud-agnostic SQL Server management base module. Manages SQL Server logins, database users,
and password rotation using the betr-io/mssql provider. Designed to be called from
cloud-specific management modules via `module "db" { providers = { mssql = mssql } }`.
No cloud provider resources are included; databases must already exist on the target server.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module provides a reusable, cloud-agnostic foundation for managing SQL Server logins
and database users. It handles two categories of users:

- **Owner users** (`create_login = true`, the default): full SQL Server logins are created
  with auto-generated, rotation-aware passwords and assigned to one or more databases with
  configurable roles (default `db_owner`).
- **Regular users** (`create_login = false`): same lifecycle but with `db_datareader` as the
  default role and managed independently from owner resources.

Password rotation is driven by `time_rotating` and `random_password` keepers, supporting
both periodic rotation (`password_rotation_period`) and on-demand reset (`force_reset`).

The module is intended to be composed into cloud-specific modules that supply the mssql
provider connection and handle secret storage (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager).

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mssql-management/releases).


```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl (cloud-specific wrapper)
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mssql-management.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "prod"
  }

  # SQL Server connection — host is the FQDN of the server, port defaults to 1433
  server_host = "myserver.database.windows.net"
  server_port = 1433

  # Map of database references (must already exist on the server)
  databases = {
    app_db = {
      name = "AppDatabase"
    }
  }

  # Map of SQL Server logins and database users
  users = {
    app_owner = {
      name         = "app_owner"          # SQL Server login name
      databases    = ["AppDatabase"]      # databases to create user in
      roles        = ["db_owner"]         # optional, default for create_login=true
      create_login = true                 # optional, default true
    }
    app_reader = {
      name         = "app_reader"
      databases    = ["AppDatabase"]
      roles        = ["db_datareader"]
      create_login = false                # no SQL Server login; user-only path
    }
  }

  # Optional password lifecycle settings
  password_rotation_period = 90   # rotate every 90 days; 0 = disabled
  force_reset              = false
}
```

## Quick Start

1. Reference this module from your cloud-specific Terragrunt stack, supplying the `mssql`
   provider via `generate "provider"` or a parent `providers` block.
2. Populate `databases` with references to pre-existing SQL Server databases.
3. Define `users` with the desired logins, target databases, and roles.
4. Run `terragrunt apply`.
5. Consume the `owner_passwords`, `owner_usernames`, `user_passwords`, and `user_usernames`
   outputs in your cloud module to store credentials in your secret manager of choice.


## Examples

See `usage` above for a complete Terragrunt example.

To force an immediate password reset without waiting for the rotation period:
```hcl
inputs = {
  force_reset = true
}
```
After the rotation completes, set `force_reset = false` to prevent further resets.

|------|---------|
| <a name="requirement_terraform"></a> [terraform](#requirement\_terraform) | >= 1.7 |
| <a name="requirement_mssql"></a> [mssql](#requirement\_mssql) | ~> 0.3 |
| <a name="requirement_random"></a> [random](#requirement\_random) | ~> 3.4 |
| <a name="requirement_time"></a> [time](#requirement\_time) | ~> 0.13 |

## Resources

| [mssql_login.owner](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/betr-io/mssql/latest/docs/resources/login) | resource |

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_databases"></a> [databases](#input\_databases) | Map of database references managed by this module. Databases must already exist. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_force_reset"></a> [force\_reset](#input\_force\_reset) | (Optional) Force password reset on next apply. Default: false. | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_password_rotation_period"></a> [password\_rotation\_period](#input\_password\_rotation\_period) | (Optional) Password rotation period in days. Default: 0. | `number` | `0` | no |
| <a name="input_server_host"></a> [server\_host](#input\_server\_host) | (Required) SQL Server hostname or IP address. Passed to the server{} block of each mssql resource. | `string` | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_server_port"></a> [server\_port](#input\_server\_port) | (Optional) SQL Server TCP port. Default: 1433. | `number` | `1433` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
| <a name="input_users"></a> [users](#input\_users) | Map of SQL Server logins and database users. See inline docs for full schema. | `any` | `{}` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_databases"></a> [databases](#output\_databases) | Map of db\_ref → { name } for all referenced databases. |
| <a name="output_owner_passwords"></a> [owner\_passwords](#output\_owner\_passwords) | Map of user\_ref → login password (sensitive) for create\_login=true users. Consumed by cloud modules for secret storage. |
| <a name="output_owner_usernames"></a> [owner\_usernames](#output\_owner\_usernames) | Map of user\_ref → SQL Server login name for create\_login=true users. |
| <a name="output_user_passwords"></a> [user\_passwords](#output\_user\_passwords) | Map of user\_ref → login password (sensitive) for create\_login=false users. |
| <a name="output_user_usernames"></a> [user\_usernames](#output\_user\_usernames) | Map of user\_ref → SQL Server login name for create\_login=false users. |
| <a name="output_users"></a> [users](#output\_users) | Map of user\_ref → { name, create\_login } for all managed users. |
